title: Which Impact event did you like more 20 N3I or 30 N4I
display_title: Which Impact event did you like more 20 N3I or 30 N4I
wikitext:
[]: Which Impact event did you like more: 2.0 N3I or 3.0 N4I?             Quote Post by WunderBah » Mon Feb 17, 2014 10:55 pm For me, it would be N4I in 3.0. Beethoven's 9th symphoney (my favorite version by far), the atmosphere, intensity, and Shinji experiencing it in horror was what made it my favorite key event in Q. I really liked N31 + Tsubasa wo Kudasai from 2.0 though. A lot!

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Mon Feb 17, 2014 11:06 pm Enjoyed both Wonder what song will be in Final (because we gonna get another one right ? )

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:12 am Absolutely N4I. There was so much more horror of "Oh god, this is an impact", and it looked quite pretty to boot.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:26 am N3I. A+, would impact again.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 18, 2014 4:28 am N4I. N3I was too centered on Shinji rescuing Rei to make us completely concentrate on what was really happening, or even totally care. But N4I is totally concentrated on conveying us the horror of what was happening : "It's an Impact, and we're all gonna to die!" Plus the whole thing was far more impressive, with the instant giant vortex, Black Moon emerging from the ground and obliterating what's left of the ruins of Tokyo-3 , thousands of FoIs and buildings floating everywhere... and at the same time being mindscrewy with the neo-NERV pyramid mysteriously floating(and later disappearing) above the Black Moon, like a cosy spectator of this whole madness(which is exactly what Gendo and Fuyu were!), the mysterious structure in the Chamber of Guf and the otherworldly screams/moaning, as if it was coming from the FoIs... It's the end of the world, and it show all its horror to us in a front seat and glorious HD! And with Beethoven!

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Tue Feb 18, 2014 4:49 am 4th impact. It was bigger, better and it had Beethoven.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Feb 18, 2014 7:11 am N4I for all the reasons Mariachi described.

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Tue Feb 18, 2014 7:47 am N4I. Most beautiful apocalyptic event since EoE. Four stars, would definitely order it again...but next time, hold off on the side of "fey boy's head exploding". I'm starting to see a pattern developing here...

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Tue Feb 18, 2014 8:58 am AngelNo13Bardiel wrote: N4I. Most beautiful apocalyptic event since EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 18, 2014 9:38 am The thing with NTI was, we were not supposed to get it was a worldwide catastrophe/think it was just a high budget beef up of EVA 01 doing scary shouting in ep 19. It's quite possible (see the CR talk about "wanting to end on a positive note") that Anno & co hadn't even decided to make it a planet-wrecking event before that earthquake; So it just generally looks like fancy property damage; The BGM seems to think Rei getting hugged is more important, and since Kaworu stops it right away/ you know the world didn't end in eps 23 or 19... And regardless of what Anno did or didn't plan, at least Gendo & Mari look fairly unconcerned, Gendo didn't expect to have to wait much longer, Fuyu didn't call him out on basically making human sacrifies of Shinji and Rei, Mari even compliments Shinji, at some point, someting must've gone waaaaaay of the rails(See ongoing speculation about everything NTI related// the various sights in NERV HQ) If it was any more impressive, it wouldn't serve the purpose of the scene. That is, it WAS impressive, but more "awesome display of power" impressive, not "epic destruction" imressive. For 4I, we all knew it was supposed to be an apocalypse, and they had even shinier graphix so... Yeah, it's really well done. You see the bits and pieces of the already ruined Tokyo3 break appart some more in glorious detail, the sheer horror of this huge sphere coming out of the ground like Slan's face in berserk, and you realize how freakin huge it is, Beethoven... There's also how overwehelmingly swift it happens. One moment Shinji pulls the spears, and the next, EVA 13 lights up, breaks upwards through the main shaft, the impact vortex spawns in a manner of seconds... All of this happened a lot more gradually in 2.0. You recognize the phenomena, but you just stare as one happens after the other, and the next moment, Asuka is already fighting the Mk 9 and there's lots of mid-air crazyness...

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:40 pm N4I: Bigger, better, Beethoven. You've tried the rest. Now try the best.

[]: Re: Which Impact event did you like more: 2.0 N3I or 3.0 N4I             Quote Post by Lumiere » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:53 pm WunderBah wrote: For me, it would be N4I in 3.0. Beethoven's 9th symphoney (my favorite version by far), the atmosphere, intensity, and Shinji experiencing it in horror was what made it my favorite key event in Q. I really liked N31 + Tsubasa wo Kudasai from 2.0 though. A lot!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:56 pm The one thing that's always struck me about N4I is the fact that, apart from the giant mushroom, nothing seems to have happened. N3I led to 3I, which wrecked the planet. N4I appears to have done pretty much bupkiss. So I'm left with one Impact that got the job done and another that's all flash and no substance. Gotta break with the pack here and vote N3I.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 18, 2014 1:16 pm Bagheera wrote: So I'm left with one Impact that got the job done

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Tue Feb 18, 2014 2:22 pm While I love everything about the ending of 2.0, if we're gonna go with an Impact as a sheer spectacle of sight and sound, I gotta say that 3.0 had the better Impact. And, while I agree that the Beethoven definitely made everything better, let's not forget the awesomeness that is "The Wrath of God in All It's Fury".

[]:             Quote Post by LegionWrex » Tue Feb 18, 2014 4:12 pm N3I by far. It came as a surprise, and music... oh god that music... fantastic. Just fantastic. And it wasn't as overblown as N4I. That being said, N4I is still awesome. I think it just went on a bit too long and was mostly there to give an awesome backdrop for the finale.

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Tue Feb 18, 2014 8:33 pm N3I. Since I know where the music was originally used, it felt out of place in 3.0. It could very well have come earlier and worked fine.

[]:             Quote Post by WunderBah » Tue Feb 18, 2014 10:28 pm Charsi wrote: N3I. Since I know where the music was originally used, it felt out of place in 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Feb 19, 2014 3:45 am Which music you're talking about : "Beethoven 9th" or "The Wrath of God in all It's Fury"? Because in Rebuild Beethoven 9th is more SEELE's leitmotiv than Kaworu's, there's even a line of the song engraved in their logo! Under that light the Beethoven song when Fourth Impact is starting is very fitting.

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Wed Feb 19, 2014 1:56 pm WunderBah wrote: I disagree, i felt the music worked effectively as an icing on the cake in order to convey N4I's atmosphere so well.
summary:
[]: Which Impact event did you like more: 2.0 N3I or 3.0 N4I?             Quote Post by WunderBah » Mon Feb 17, 2014 10:55 pm For me, it would be N4I in 3.0. Beethoven's 9th symphoney (my favorite version by far), the atmosphere, intensity, and Shinji experiencing it in horror was what made it my favorite key event in Q. I really liked N31 + Tsubasa wo Kudasai from 2.0 though. A lot!

[]:        &nb
